GitHub Organizations and Team Management for Open Source Projects

Any developer will end up touching open source at some point — and if you're running your own project that's growing, knowing how to use GitHub Organizations and Teams becomes pretty important. This post walks through creating a free Organization, building a Team inside it, adding members, assigning repos to the team, and configuring per-team repository roles (Read / Triage / Write / Maintain / Admin).
JavaScript Core Concepts (73) - Promise - Why Do We Even Need Promises?

Time to get into Promises. Honestly, you've probably been using them in real projects already — you just didn't realize that thing you were calling was wrapped in a Promise. This post walks through why Promises exist and how they compare to the old jQuery-style async code.

Postman is one of those tools that pays for itself within the first hour. Before integrating an API into your actual project, just test it in Postman — saves an enormous amount of debug time later. This post is a quick start — installing Postman, creating requests, organizing into collections, setting up environment variables, generating snippet code for your codebase, exporting/importing collections, and running automated tests across a collection.
Time to talk about something really important in Nuxt.js — the lifecycle. It heavily affects your SSR output and your SEO.

A quick tour of browser extensions I actually use as a developer. Covers translation, color picking (ColorZilla), server location (Flagfox / IP Domain Country Flag), font identification (Fonts Ninja), tech stack detection (Wappalyzer, whatruns), CMS detection, Gmail notifications, JSON viewer, SEO analysis, and HTML outliner. Firefox-first since that's my main browser, with Chrome links where available.
What Is a Promise in JavaScript? And Why You Really Need to Get It

This post walks through what a Promise actually is in JavaScript, plus the Async/Await syntax that builds on top of it. We'll cover how to write a Promise, the then/catch chain, the static methods like Promise.all and Promise.race, the three Promise states, and how async/await cleans all of it up into something far easier to read.
Nuxt.js 2.x Hands-On Handbook (4) — How Nuxt.js Works
This chapter is a quick chat about how Nuxt.js actually works.
A Field Guide to JavaScript Error Types (So You Can Debug Faster)

A quick reference for the different kinds of errors JavaScript throws at you. We stare at red error messages all day during development, so if you can recognize the error type at a glance, it actually makes debugging a lot easier. This post walks through SyntaxError, RangeError, ReferenceError, TypeError, and URIError with real examples.
What's an API? And What's a RESTful API?

RESTful API is a phrase you hear constantly as a frontend dev. But what's actually behind it? This post walks through what an API is (with a postman + painter analogy), then builds up to RESTful API as a design style — the conventions around plural vs. singular resource paths, HTTP methods, URL casing, versioning, and why using REST conventions makes your code easier for other devs to read.
